name: Louie Bellson
abstract: Louie Bellson (born Luigi Paolino Alfredo Francesco Antonio Balassoni, July 6, 1924 – February 14, 2009), was an American jazz drummer, composer, arranger, bandleader, and jazz educator. He is credited with pioneering the use of two bass drums.

interviews.title: Louie Bellson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Duke Ellington's band. This was a great band, this particular band was a great band. And this was -- band was Louie Bellson, Wendell Marshall, I believe, Shorty Baker, Cooke, what's his name.

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: Well Louie Bellson replaced me with Benny Goodman. He had just been discharged from the army, so when he was discharged, he replaced me and I went back to my studio work and my drum school.

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: I hired big bands, small groups. I had Michel Petrucciani. I had Woody Herman. I had Dave Brubeck. I had Freddie Hubbard. I had Louie Bellson. I had the Jimmy Heath Quartet.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: So we put Bob Haggart and Louie Bellson in the band... And then I joined the great legion of people who were let go of a Benny Goodman band. My own band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Most
about_quotes.evidence: I had Louie Bellson on drums, Percy Heath and who else, Urbie Green and Bob Dorough... That was a thing that Charlie Mingus put out on Debut Records.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sam Most interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Louis Bellson and Charlie Shavers co-led a combo in Moline... Louis Bellson, Buddy de Franco, Charlie Shavers, Hank Jones and myself.
